stories.headline: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
stories.summary: Leah Stewart, 34, had one of her arms amputated after she was bitten while swimming at Coogee Beach.

stories.headline: Woman attacked by shark at Coogee beach wakes briefly from coma to say three words
stories.summary: ‘I love you,’ Leah Stewart told her mother and partner after being extubated on TuesdayFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA woman attacked by a shark at Sydney’s Coogee beach has uttered her first words after waking from a coma 10 days after sustaining her injuries.“I love you,” Leah Stewart told her mother and partner after being extubated on Tuesday. stories.headline: Trump claims US recouped Venezuela war costs 28 times over
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump claimed the United States has recovered its costs 28 times over through oil extraction.

stories.headline: US Senate votes to pause Iran war in rare rebuke to Trump
stories.summary: US Senate votes to pause Iran war in rare rebuke to Trump

stories.headline: Bondi Beach shooting hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on his father
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed tackled one of the gunmen who killed 15 people at a Jewish event in December.

stories.headline: Qatar says US-Iran hotline essential to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed says Gulf state will resume normal LNG production within weeks

stories.headline: Indian Tech’s Nifty Share Shrinks to Record Low on AI Worries
stories.summary: India’s software exporters are steadily losing their sway on the country’s stock market as concerns over artificial intelligence-led disruption trigger a prolonged selloff in the sector.

stories.headline: AirPods Maker Luxshare Gauges Interest for $3 Billion Hong Kong Listing
stories.summary: Electronics manufacturer Luxshare Precision Industry Co. has started gauging interest for a Hong Kong listing that may raise about $3 billion, according to people familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified to discuss a private matter, moving toward what could be one of the city’s biggest deals this year.

stories.headline: Kenyan Health Minister orders halt of construction of US Ebola facility
stories.summary: Kenya’s Health Minister has stopped the construction of a US Ebola facility in Kenya.

stories.headline: Disinfectant brand apologises after ‘toxic men’ advert backfires in China
stories.summary: Dettol has apologised after an advert in China intended to challenge sexism was accused of reinforcing it.

stories.headline: Nvidia’s banned AI chips double in price on China’s black market
stories.summary: US crackdown on illicit exports has made it riskier, harder and more expensive to buy tech giant’s processors

stories.headline: SoftBank Seeks Stake in Japan’s Top Utility to Power AI Boom
stories.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. is looking to invest in Japan’s biggest power utility to help secure the electricity needed to expand in artificial intelligence, the company’s chief executive officer said.

stories.headline: Leveraged Korea ETFs Sold Estimated $6 Billion of Shares in Rout
stories.summary: Leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. or SK Hynix Inc. were likely forced to sell a combined $6 billion of the Korean chipmakers’ shares Tuesday to maintain leverage ratios, underscoring how such products can amplify market moves, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

stories.headline: Is the AI Bubble Bursting? Not Yet, Says Dan Farley
stories.summary: A tentative rebound in Asian equities following Tuesday’s global tech-led rout failed to hold as a fresh bout of selling hit the sector. State Street Investment Management's Dan Farley discusses whether the AI rally still has room to go. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Qatar Sees Most LNG Output Returning Within Weeks, FT Says
stories.summary: Qatar plans to return to normal liquefied natural gas output within weeks from the undamaged parts of its facility, Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al-Thani said in an interview with the Financial Times.

stories.headline: Korean Stocks Swing After Rout as Caution Grows on AI Earnings
stories.summary: South Korean stocks swung between gains and losses as investors debated the outlook for chipmakers’ earnings and the sustainability of this year’s world-beating rally.

stories.headline: EU Rides Out Budget Spat Without Spooking Markets or Ratings
stories.summary: The European Union’s regime for monitoring public finances has arguably just passed its toughest health test since a revamp after the pandemic.

stories.headline: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group Seeks Fresh Bond Extension as Refinancing Drags
stories.summary: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group has offered bondholders a 30-basis point fee in exchange for approving another extension on debt owed by its unit, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: China Unfazed by World’s Ire on Trade as Premier Touts Openness
stories.summary: Beijing’s No. 2 official played down worries about the disruption wrought by his country’s surging exports on the global economy, championing the benefits provided to the world by China’s development.

stories.headline: Double, Double Toil and Bubble Trouble
stories.summary: This probably isn’t when it bursts, but there’s room for plenty of over-leveraged damage.

stories.headline: China's Premier Li Says Beijing Remains Committed to Openness
stories.summary: Chinese Premier Li Qiang delivered a special address at the Summer Davos forum in Dalian on Wednesday. He said Beijing will continue to expand domestic demand and accelerate the large-scale application of new technologies. Bloomberg's Minmin Low reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Copper Holds Losses as Dollar and Hawkish Fed Pressure Metals
stories.summary: Copper and industrial metals largely held Tuesday’s slump as a stronger dollar and the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance weighed on the demand outlook.

stories.headline: Local Republican Wins Primary for Nancy Mace’s House Seat in South Carolina
stories.summary: Jenny Costa Honeycutt, a Charleston County councilwoman and lawyer, topped a Republican rival in a runoff primary election for a seat that leans to the right but could be competitive in November.

stories.headline: Protesters Accused of Antifa Ties Sentenced to Up to 100 Years in ICE Attack
stories.summary: The penalties, issued in an attack where a police officer was shot, dwarfed those given to Jan. 6 rioters and appeared to signal that at least some courts will deal aggressively with ICE protesters.

stories.headline: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls

stories.headline: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction

stories.headline: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back

stories.summary: China is choking shipments of some critical minerals to Japan, a slowdown that’s hurting companies and prompting calls for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to find a diplomatic off-ramp with Beijing.

stories.summary: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicideCampaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her.Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. stories.headline: Von der Leyen to visit Pashinyan in Yerevan to build on Armenia’s pro-EU vote
stories.summary: As the EU rolls out a support package for Armenia, European Commission President von der Leyen is set to meet re-elected Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan after he secured a decisive pro-West mandate from the Armenian people, sources confirmed to Euronews.

stories.headline: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion

stories.headline: Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation
stories.summary: A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using ‘self-driving’ technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home.

stories.headline: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

stories.headline: Protest led by India's 'cockroach party' enters its third day
stories.summary: The group has said the protest will continue until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan steps down.

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.headline: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on

stories.headline: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities

stories.headline: Half of France under red heat alert as alcohol banned at street music festival

stories.headline: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
stories.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

stories.headline: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
stories.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.
